title: Mandatory Publishing, Returns, Alternate Exchanges, and Route Audits · Contract Lens: Alternate Return Tradeoff
summary: The service publishes with mandatory false, treats confirms as proof of routing, attaches the return listener after publishing, and retries unroutable messages forever without fixing topology. Planned artifact: Add and verify the alternate return tradeoff increment in unroutable publication detection and ownership record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose mandatory returns, alternate exchanges, or both from publisher feedback, broker custody, operational ownership, and observability needs.
Classify unroutable messages as contract, deployment, tenant, or malicious failures and choose alert, quarantine, reject, or repair ownership.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Alternate Return Tradeoff · Theory Alternate Return Tradeoff
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 6483 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to keep mandatory return, alternate custody, confirmation, and route uncertainty separate; retain a screen-reader-readable delivery and ownership trace; then introduce a sequence gap for one partition while another stays healthy. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose mandatory returns, alternate exchanges, or both from publisher feedback, broker custody, operational ownership, and observability needs.
